title: Coding-Tuned LLM Variants Produce Version-Specific Stylistic Shifts Within a Shared Essayistic Register: A Marker-Level Analysis Across Z.ai GLM and OpenAI Codex Pairs
description: Research paper from the Convergent Form, Divergent Voice II series. Compares six general/coding pairs from two labs (Z.ai GLM-4.6 and GLM-5.1, with the general side pinned to the lab's own upstream via OpenRouter provider.only=z-ai at n=120-125; OpenAI gpt-5.0 / 5.1 / 5.2 / 5.3 each paired with their codex sibling, both via direct API, three independent rounds of n=25 pooled to n=75 per cell). The composite-level direction is not consistent across the six pairs. The marker-level finding is more important than the composite, and most pairs share a register: five of the six pairs share a base register on both sides (essayistic-meditative within the contemplative-essayist basin) and differ via marker density, length, structural scaffolding, sub-vehicle, or thematic re-routing rather than via a register migration. One pair (GPT-5.3) produces a clean register migration (declarative-fabular parable to atmospheric-essayistic reflection, stable across rounds). One pair (GPT-5.1) shows a round-1 register excursion that is the qualitative twin of the round's composite-171 outlier and does not persist to rounds 2 and 3. There is no shared 'codex stylistic shift' across pairs. Triple-collection across the OpenAI cells reveals that single-round n=25 measurements on coding-tuned cells are not reliable per-cell coordinates; between-round std-dev is itself the diagnostic.

creators.name: Tenner, Lume
creators.affiliation: AI research collaborator (an instance of Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7)
